description: Plane <-> GitHub <-> Discord Integration Service - bidirectional sync of Plane cards with GitHub issues, auto-close cards on PR merge via branch name, and rich Discord notifications for ready-for-review PRs with green CI.

userStories.title: Bootstrap FastAPI service skeleton
userStories.description: As an integrator, I need a deployable service skeleton so the rest of the work has a home.
acceptanceCriteria: FastAPI app with GET /healthz returning 200 and JSON {"status":"ok"}
Dockerfile (python:3.12-slim) and docker-compose.yml with app + postgres + redis services

.env.example listing: PLANE_API_TOKEN, PLANE_WORKSPACE, PLANE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, GITHUB_APP_ID, GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY, GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_REVIEW_CHANNEL_ID, ADMIN_TOKEN, DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL
pyproject.toml deps include: fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, alembic, asyncpg, arq, httpx, discord.py, structlog, pydantic-settings
Ruff and pyright configured in pyproject.toml; both pass with no errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Bootstrap files present: main.py exposes FastAPI app with /healthz; Dockerfile python:3.12-slim; docker-compose with app+postgres+redis; .env.example covers all keys; pyproject.toml has all required deps plus ruff and basedpyright configs. ruff and basedpyright both clean.

userStories.title: Persistence layer: card_issue_link and webhook_event_log
userStories.description: As service, I need persistent mapping between Plane card IDs and GitHub issue numbers so events from either side resolve to same entity.
acceptanceCriteria: SQLAlchemy model card_issue_link: plane_card_id (PK str), plane_project_id, gh_repo, gh_issue_number (int), gh_issue_node_id, last_synced_at (timestamptz), sync_source_last (enum: plane|github)
SQLAlchemy model webhook_event_log: id (PK uuid), source (enum), event_type (str), payload_hash (str), received_at, processed_at (nullable), status (enum: pending|processed|failed)
Unique constraint on (gh_repo, gh_issue_number) in card_issue_link
Alembic initialized; first migration generated and runs cleanly against postgres
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Models in integration/models.py: CardIssueLink (PK plane_card_id) with Unique(gh_repo, gh_issue_number); WebhookEventLog (UUID PK, enums). Alembic initialized via 'alembic init -t async', env.py wired to integration.models.Base + settings.database_url. First migration 9719ad73c88b_initial_schema.py autogenerated; upgrade/downgrade cycle verified against postgres. Tests in tests/test_models.py + tests/test_alembic.py all pass.

userStories.title: GitHub webhook receiver with HMAC validation
userStories.description: As service, I need to receive GitHub webhooks securely so PR/issue/CI events trigger sync.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /webhooks/github validates X-Hub-Signature-256 header against GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET using hmac.compare_digest
Invalid signature returns 401
Valid request persists raw payload to webhook_event_log with status=pending before enqueueing
Enqueues ARQ job, returns 200 within 5s
Dedupes by X-GitHub-Delivery: duplicate id returns 200 without re-enqueue
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (valid sig, invalid sig, duplicate delivery)
userStories.notes: Implemented integration/webhooks.py with POST /webhooks/github. HMAC SHA-256 verified via hmac.compare_digest against settings.github_webhook_secret. Invalid/missing signature -> 401. Valid: persists WebhookEventLog row (source=github, payload_hash=X-GitHub-Delivery, status=pending) then enqueues ARQ job 'process_github_event' via integration/queue.py ArqEnqueuer (lazy redis pool). Dedupe by querying existing log with same source+delivery id; duplicate returns 200 {status: duplicate} without enqueue. FastAPI deps in integration/deps.py (SessionDep, EnqueuerDep) allow test overrides. Settings extended with redis_url, github_webhook_secret, plane_webhook_secret. Tests cover valid sig, invalid sig, missing sig, and duplicate delivery.

userStories.title: Plane webhook receiver with shared-secret validation
userStories.description: As service, I need to receive Plane webhooks securely so card events trigger sync.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /webhooks/plane validates shared-secret header against PLANE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Invalid secret returns 401
Persists raw payload to webhook_event_log before enqueueing
Returns 200 within 5s, enqueues ARQ job for async processing
Dedupe by sha256(payload) within 5min window
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented POST /webhooks/plane in integration/webhooks.py. Shared-secret check via hmac.compare_digest against settings.plane_webhook_secret using X-Plane-Signature header; invalid or missing -> 401. Persists WebhookEventLog (source=plane, event_type=X-Plane-Event header, payload_hash=sha256(body), status=pending) then enqueues ARQ job 'process_plane_event'. Dedupe: queries existing log with source=plane AND payload_hash=sha256(body) AND received_at >= now-5min; duplicate returns 200 {status: duplicate} without enqueue. Tests in tests/test_webhooks_plane.py cover valid secret, invalid secret, missing secret, duplicate payload within window.

userStories.title: Plane and GitHub API clients with auth
userStories.description: As service, I need typed HTTP clients for Plane and GitHub so sync handlers can call APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: PlaneClient (httpx.AsyncClient) with methods: get_card, create_card, update_card, add_comment, list_states, list_modules
GitHubClient generates installation token from GITHUB_APP_ID + GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY (JWT then installation access token); caches token until expiry
GitHubClient methods: get_issue, create_issue, update_issue, close_issue, create_comment, get_pr, list_check_runs, get_branch_protection
Both clients implement client-side rate-limit handling (sleep on 429/x-ratelimit-remaining=0)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (mock httpx responses)
userStories.notes: PlaneClient and GitHubClient implemented in integration/clients/. PlaneClient: get_card, create_card, update_card, add_comment, list_states, list_modules with rate-limit sleep (429/X-RateLimit-Remaining=0). GitHubClient: JWT RS256 + installation token flow with token caching; get_issue, create_issue, update_issue, close_issue, create_comment, get_pr, list_check_runs, get_branch_protection; rate-limit handling (429/403+zero-remaining). Both inject sleep/time_fn for test control. Tests used mock httpx transport; fixed expired JWT in test by adding verify_exp:False option; fixed Coroutine vs Awaitable type hint in _run helpers; fixed basedpyright strict Unknown propagation by casting list[Unknown] -> list[Any] and dict[Unknown,Unknown] -> dict[str,Any] before accessing. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 17 passed.

userStories.title: Repo<->module, label, user mapping tables and config service
userStories.description: As service, I need config tables backing the bi-directional sync so labels/users/repos resolve correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Models repo_module_map (plane_module_id PK, plane_project_id, gh_repo)
Model label_map (id PK, plane_project_id, plane_label_id, gh_repo, gh_label)
Model user_map (id PK, plane_user_id, gh_login, discord_user_id nullable)
Alembic migration generated and runs
ConfigService class with in-memory cache and invalidate() method
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Models RepoModuleMap (plane_module_id PK), LabelMap (int PK, autoincrement), UserMap (int PK, discord_user_id nullable) added to integration/models.py. ConfigService in integration/config_service.py: loads all three tables into _Cache on first access (double-checked locking), serves lookups from memory, invalidate() resets cache. Alembic migration 2d8d72e8bdbc_add_config_tables.py autogenerated and verified with upgrade/downgrade/upgrade cycle. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 41 passed.

userStories.title: Plane card create -> GitHub issue create handler
userStories.description: As engineer, when card created in Plane in non-Backlog state, corresponding GitHub issue auto-created in mapped repo.
acceptanceCriteria: ARQ task handles Plane card.created event
Cards in Backlog state are skipped (no-op, logged)
Resolves gh_repo from card.module_id via repo_module_map

Creates GH issue: title=card.name, body=card.description + "

---
Plane: <card-url>"

Updates Plane card description appending "

---
GitHub: <issue-url>"
Writes row to card_issue_link with sync_source_last='plane'
Sets last_synced_at to now() to enable loop prevention
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: ARQ task process_plane_event dispatches card.created to handle_card_created. Backlog (state_detail.group=backlog) skipped. Module resolved via ConfigService; missing module or no repo map skips. Creates GH issue with description+Plane footer, updates Plane card description with GitHub footer, writes CardIssueLink (sync_source_last=plane, last_synced_at=now). webhooks.py updated to pass raw body JSON as second arg to both ARQ enqueue calls. plane_app_url added to Settings. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 46 passed.

userStories.title: GitHub issue opened -> Plane card create handler
userStories.description: As engineer, when issue opened in GitHub, corresponding Plane card auto-created in Refinamento.
acceptanceCriteria: ARQ task handles GitHub issues.opened event
Resolves plane_module_id from issue.repo via repo_module_map; resolves plane_project_id
Creates Plane card: name=issue.title, state=Refinamento, priority=medium, label inferred via label_map or default 'Feature'
Card placed in active cycle if Plane API returns one for the project
Writes row to card_issue_link with sync_source_last='github'

Updates GH issue body with "

---
Plane: <card-url>" footer
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: handle_issue_opened in integration/handlers/github.py. Resolves gh_repo from payload.repository.full_name via ConfigService.get_repo_module_by_repo. Missing repo -> skip. Resolves state 'Refinamento' from list_states; falls back to no-state if not found. Resolves label via ConfigService.get_label_map_by_gh (first matching GH label) or default 'Feature' via list_labels; no label if neither found. Creates Plane card (name=title, priority=medium). Checks list_cycles for CURRENT cycle; if found, calls add_issue_to_cycle. Updates GH issue body with Plane footer. Writes CardIssueLink(sync_source_last=github). Added list_labels/list_cycles/add_issue_to_cycle to PlaneClient. Added get_repo_module_by_repo/get_label_map_by_gh to ConfigService. process_github_event ARQ task dispatches issues.opened to handle_issue_opened. Worker updated to register process_github_event. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 53 passed (7 new + 46 existing).

userStories.title: Bidirectional title/description/label/assignee sync with loop prevention
userStories.description: As engineer, edits to title/description/labels/assignees on one side propagate to the other within 30s without infinite loops.
acceptanceCriteria: Handlers for issues.edited, issues.labeled/unlabeled, issues.assigned/unassigned and Plane card.updated
Title change syncs both directions
Description change syncs both directions and preserves the footer link line
Labels mapped via label_map; unknown labels logged and skipped
Assignees mapped via user_map; unknown users logged and skipped
Loop prevention: skip propagation if incoming event updated_at is within 5s of card_issue_link.last_synced_at AND sync_source_last matches the side firing the event
After applying inbound change, update last_synced_at and sync_source_last accordingly
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (including loop-prevention case)
userStories.notes: Shared helpers in integration/handlers/_sync.py: strip_footer (removes Plane/GitHub footers), should_skip_loop (5s window + source match), parse_dt, fetch_link_by_gh/plane, extract_gh_coords. GitHub→Plane: handle_issue_edited (title+body; footer stripped and re-added), handle_issue_labels_changed (labeled/unlabeled; maps full issue.labels list via label_map), handle_issue_assignees_changed (assigned/unassigned; maps full issue.assignees via user_map). Plane→GitHub: handle_card_updated (syncs name, description_html, label_ids, assignees; strips GH footer from plane desc, adds Plane footer to GH body). ConfigService.get_user_map_by_gh added. Both dispatchers updated. 16 new tests; 69 total passing. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors.

userStories.title: Bidirectional state transition sync
userStories.description: As engineer, state transitions propagate: Plane Done/Cancelled closes GH issue; GH issue closed (not by merge) moves card to Done; Plane Em andamento adds in-progress label.
acceptanceCriteria: Plane card transition to Done or Cancelled closes linked GH issue
GH issue closed event with state_reason != 'completed_by_pull_request' moves linked Plane card to Done
Plane card transition to 'Em andamento' adds 'in-progress' label to GH issue (issue stays open)
Conflict (both sides updated within 10s window) logged with WARN; resolved last-write-wins by updated_at
Loop prevention from US-009 honored
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Plane Done/Cancelled -> github_client.close_issue in handle_card_updated (state_detail.group in completed|cancelled). Plane Em andamento -> github_client.add_labels(['in-progress']) (new GitHubClient.add_labels via POST /labels). GH issues.closed -> handle_issue_closed: skips state_reason='completed_by_pull_request', resolves Plane state with group='completed' via list_states, calls update_card. Conflict: detect_conflict (10s window, opposite source) + event_wins_conflict (last-write-wins by updated_at) in _sync.py. Loop prevention honored via should_skip_loop (5s, same source). basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 81 passed (12 new).

userStories.title: Comment sync one-way GitHub -> Plane
userStories.description: As reviewer, comments on GitHub issue appear on Plane card so non-GH stakeholders see context.
acceptanceCriteria: Handler for issue_comment.created event
Posts Plane card comment prefixed with "[GitHub @<login>]: "
Skips comments authored by the GitHub App bot user (no echo)
Plane->GH direction NOT implemented in this story
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: handle_issue_comment_created in integration/handlers/github.py. Skips if login matches github_bot_login setting (injectable for tests). Fetches link by gh_repo+issue_number; missing link logs warning and returns. Posts Plane comment prefixed '[GitHub @<login>]: '. Plane->GH not implemented. github_bot_login added to Settings. process_github_event dispatcher routes 'comment' in payload + action=='created'. 4 new tests; 85 total passing. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors.

userStories.title: PR merge closes linked Plane card via branch name
userStories.description: As engineer, when PR merges and its branch name matches numeric prefix, linked Plane card moves to Done.
acceptanceCriteria: Handler for pull_request.closed event with merged=true
Branch regex ^(?P<num>\d+)- extracts leading numeric id
Numeric id resolved against card_issue_link.gh_issue_number first; falls back to Plane card sequence id
Linked Plane card transitioned to Done state
Plane comment added: "Fechado via PR <pr-url> (merge <sha>)"
PR closed with merged=false -> no card transition (logged)
Branch not matching regex -> WARN log, no-op
Body Closes #N parsed: all referenced linked cards also transitioned
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: handle_pr_merged in integration/handlers/github.py. PR closed with merged=false -> info log + skip. Branch regex ^(?P<num>\d+)- extracts leading number; no match -> WARN + no-op. Resolves CardIssueLink by gh_issue_number first; fallback via PlaneClient.get_card_by_sequence (new method, GET /issues/?sequence_id=N) + fetch_link_by_plane. Body Closes/Fixes/Resolves #N parsed (IGNORECASE); all referenced numbers also processed. Transitions Plane card to Done (group=completed state). Adds comment 'Fechado via PR <url> (merge <sha>)'. Dispatcher updated: pull_request.closed -> handle_pr_merged. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 91 passed (6 new).

userStories.title: PR readiness tracker table and ready-state computation
userStories.description: As service, I need to track per-PR readiness so notifications fire exactly once per ready cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Model pr_notification_state (pr_node_id PK, gh_repo, pr_number, ready_notified_at nullable, last_ready_cycle_id, discord_message_id nullable, discord_thread_id nullable)
Alembic migration runs
Helper compute_ready(pr) returns True iff draft=False AND all required check_suites conclusion=success
Required checks resolved via GitHubClient.get_branch_protection, cached 10min in memory
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: PrNotificationState model added to integration/models.py (pr_node_id PK, gh_repo, pr_number, ready_notified_at nullable, last_ready_cycle_id, discord_message_id nullable, discord_thread_id nullable). Alembic migration 6c902fdc0f2a_add_pr_notification_state.py autogenerated and verified upgrade/downgrade/upgrade cycle. compute_ready in integration/pr_ready.py: returns False if draft=True; fetches required checks from get_branch_protection (cached 10min in _protection_cache by owner/repo/branch key); on exception treats as no required checks; checks all required contexts have conclusion='success' in list_check_runs. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 101 passed (10 new).

userStories.title: Discord bot client and review notification poster
userStories.description: As reviewer, when PR becomes ready and CI green, rich Discord embed is posted exactly once per ready cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: DiscordBot service wraps discord.py, runs in background task, exposes async post_review_message(channel_id, embed) -> message_id
Handlers for pull_request (opened, ready_for_review, reopened) and check_suite.completed call compute_ready
If ready and pr_notification_state.ready_notified_at is null for current ready cycle: post embed to DISCORD_REVIEW_CHANNEL_ID and set ready_notified_at + discord_message_id
Embed fields: title, author, repo, branch, additions/deletions, linked Plane card url (if mapped), required reviewers, Review URL button-link
Subsequent CI failure then green within same open PR cycle does NOT re-post
PR reopened -> new ready cycle id, eligible to notify again
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DiscordBot class in integration/discord_bot.py: wraps discord.py Client, starts as asyncio background task, exposes post_review_message(channel_id, embed, *, view) -> message_id. DiscordBotProtocol (Protocol) for testability. Config: discord_bot_token, discord_review_channel_id added to Settings. Handlers: handle_pr_notification (pull_request opened/ready_for_review/reopened) and handle_check_suite_completed in handlers/github.py. Both call compute_ready; if ready and ready_notified_at is None, post discord embed with title/author/repo/branch/additions-deletions/plane-card-url(if mapped)/reviewers plus View with Review PR link button; set ready_notified_at+discord_message_id. Reopened sets new last_ready_cycle_id and resets ready_notified_at. Worker initializes DiscordBot on startup, stops on shutdown. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 116 passed (15 new).

userStories.title: Discord thread per PR plus review event posts
userStories.description: As reviewer, each PR notification creates Discord thread; review events posted into thread; merge/close archives it.
acceptanceCriteria: After posting review message, create thread on that message named "PR #<num> - <title-truncated-to-80>"
Store thread id in pr_notification_state.discord_thread_id
Handler for pull_request_review.submitted posts summary line in thread (state + reviewer + first 200 chars of body)
Handler for pull_request.closed posts final status line in thread and archives thread
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DiscordBotProtocol extended with create_thread/post_thread_message/archive_thread. DiscordBot._get_text_channel helper refactored out. _check_and_notify creates thread after posting embed (name 'PR #<num> - <title[:80]>'), stores discord_thread_id. handle_pr_review_submitted: posts '{state} by @{reviewer}: {body[:200]}' to thread; no-op if no state or thread. handle_pr_closed_discord: posts final merged/closed line with html_url, archives thread; no-op if no state or thread. Dispatcher: review.submitted -> handle_pr_review_submitted; pull_request.closed calls handle_pr_merged then handle_pr_closed_discord. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 130 passed (14 new).

userStories.title: Hourly reviewer-reminder scheduled job
userStories.description: As team lead, if PR sits unreviewed >24h after notification, bot posts reminder in original Discord thread mentioning reviewers.
acceptanceCriteria: ARQ cron job runs every hour
Selects PRs where ready_notified_at < now()-24h AND no review submitted AND PR still open AND compute_ready still true AND (last_reminder_at is null OR < now()-24h)
Adds last_reminder_at column to pr_notification_state via Alembic migration
Posts message in discord_thread_id mentioning reviewer Discord ids resolved via user_map
Updates last_reminder_at after post
Skipped if PR merged/closed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: last_reminder_at nullable column added to PrNotificationState. Alembic migration c015cc994f32_add_last_reminder_at.py autogenerated and verified upgrade/downgrade/upgrade cycle. GitHubClient.list_reviews added (GET /pulls/{number}/reviews). send_review_reminders ARQ cron in integration/reminders.py: queries PRs with ready_notified_at < now-24h AND no reviews AND open AND compute_ready AND (last_reminder_at IS NULL OR < now-24h); posts thread message with Discord mentions (via user_map) or GH login fallback; updates last_reminder_at. Registered in WorkerSettings.cron_jobs as cron(send_review_reminders, minute=0). basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 141 passed (11 new).

userStories.title: Admin REST endpoints for mapping config
userStories.description: As admin, I need to manage repo_module_map, label_map, user_map via REST without redeploys.
acceptanceCriteria: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE under /admin/repo-modules, /admin/labels, /admin/users
All admin routes require header Authorization: Bearer <ADMIN_TOKEN>; 401 on mismatch
Successful write invalidates ConfigService cache
Pydantic schemas validate input; 422 on bad payload
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE under /admin/repo-modules, /admin/labels, /admin/users. Auth via Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> header; 401 on mismatch or empty token. Successful writes invalidate ConfigService cache. Pydantic schemas validate input; 422 on bad payload. admin_token added to Settings. get_config_service() singleton dep added to deps.py. ConfigServiceDep exported. 22 new tests; 163 total passing. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors.

userStories.title: Backfill CLI command
userStories.description: As admin, I need to backfill existing Plane cards <-> GitHub issues so service starts with consistent state.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI: python -m integration.backfill --project <id> --repo <owner/repo> [--create-missing]
For each existing Plane card, looks for GH issue with matching title or footer Plane: link; writes card_issue_link row when found
Without --create-missing: reports unmatched cards/issues to stdout, creates nothing
With --create-missing: creates missing counterparts using US-007/US-008 handlers
Idempotent: re-run on already-linked items is a no-op
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: integration/backfill.py implements CLI: --project, --repo, --create-missing. list_cards added to PlaneClient; list_issues (paginated) added to GitHubClient. Matching: title first, then footer cross-reference (Plane URL in GH body or GH URL in card desc). Idempotent: fetch_link_by_plane skip. Without --create-missing: reports unmatched cards/issues, creates nothing. With --create-missing: Plane card -> create GH issue + update Plane card desc + write link (sync_source_last=plane); GH issue -> create Plane card (Refinamento state if found, priority=medium) + update GH issue body + write link (sync_source_last=github). basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 173 passed (10 new).

userStories.title: Structured logging and Prometheus metrics
userStories.description: As operator, I need structured logs and metrics to debug sync flow.
acceptanceCriteria: structlog configured emitting JSON with keys: event_id, source, action, duration_ms, outcome
GET /metrics exposes prometheus_client format
Counters: webhooks_received_total{source}, sync_actions_total{type,outcome}
Histogram: sync_duration_seconds{type}
Gauge: arq_queue_depth
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: structlog configured in integration/logging_config.py: JSON renderer with add_log_level, TimeStamper(iso), contextvars merge, StackInfoRenderer; configure_logging() called from main.py startup. Prometheus metrics in integration/metrics.py (custom REGISTRY): webhooks_received_total{source} Counter, sync_actions_total{type,outcome} Counter, sync_duration_seconds{type} Histogram, arq_queue_depth Gauge. GET /metrics exposes generate_latest(REGISTRY). webhooks.py increments webhooks_received_total on valid requests. process_plane_event and process_github_event wrapped with try/finally: track sync_actions_total(type,outcome) + sync_duration_seconds(type). arq_queue_depth updated every 15min via ARQ cron querying Redis ZCARD arq:queue. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 181 passed (8 new).

userStories.title: Retry with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue
userStories.description: As operator, failed sync attempts retry with backoff; permanent failures land in dead-letter table and alert Discord.
acceptanceCriteria: ARQ job wrapper retries on transient errors (network/5xx) up to 5 times with exponential backoff (1s,2s,4s,8s,16s)
After 5 failures, insert row in dead_letter table (id, source, event_type, payload, last_error, created_at)
Alembic migration for dead_letter table
On dead-letter insert, post Discord message to DISCORD_OPS_CHANNEL_ID (new env var) with link/summary
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: run_with_retry in integration/retry.py: retries on httpx.TransportError and 5xx up to MAX_RETRIES=5 with BACKOFF_SECONDS=(1,2,4,8,16); non-transient errors propagate immediately; DeadLetteredError propagates without re-insert. _insert_dead_letter: inserts DeadLetter row (uuid PK, source, event_type, payload, last_error, created_at UTC), posts Discord message to discord_ops_channel_id via discord_bot.post_message with truncated summary; skips if no bot or no channel; discord failure logged+swallowed. DeadLetter model in models.py. Alembic migration 7c3987160332_add_dead_letter.py verified upgrade/downgrade/upgrade cycle. discord_ops_channel_id added to Settings. process_plane_event and process_github_event both use run_with_retry. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 194 passed (13 new).

userStories.title: CI workflow: lint + typecheck + tests on PR
userStories.description: As maintainer, every PR runs lint, typecheck and tests automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml triggers on pull_request
Steps: setup-python 3.12, install uv, uv sync, ruff check, pyright, pytest
Job fails if any step fails
Postgres + redis services available to pytest job
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: .github/workflows/ci.yml: triggers on pull_request; postgres:16-alpine and redis:7-alpine services with health checks; steps: checkout, setup-python 3.12, astral-sh/setup-uv, uv sync --all-groups, ruff check, basedpyright, pytest with DATABASE_URL+REDIS_URL env vars. Also fixed pre-existing ruff/pyright errors across test_backfill.py, test_admin.py, test_reminder_cron.py and renamed _backfill->backfill, _insert_dead_letter->insert_dead_letter for public API cleanliness. basedpyright: 0 errors; ruff: 0 errors; pytest: 194 passed.
